review_text: The mini-guide kept saying "24-word seed" in one spot and "25-word" elsewhere. As a Monero newbie that scared me. Which is it?
Metal plate is thin and flexes when I try to stamp letters. Edges felt a bit sharp too. Not super confidence-inspiring for something protecting my savings.
QR code in the booklet goes to a 404 page. I couldn’t access the promised tutorial and had to search YouTube.
Screws for the letter tiles strip easily and one was missing. I ended up improvising with tape which defeats the purpose.
The guide never clearly warned me not to take a photo of the seed. It hints at privacy but for beginners it should be big and bold.
Package arrived dented and the stainless plate had scratches. Protective film was half peeled off. QC seems weak.
Font size in the printed guide is tiny. I had to use my phone flashlight to read the steps on air-gapped setup.
The included marker smears on the paper seed card. If it gets damp it becomes unreadable. Not great for long-term storage.
The booklet mentions payment IDs for exchanges without saying they’re deprecated. Totally confusing for a newcomer.
No practice card. I was afraid to stamp the real seed and make a mistake. Include a dummy card or plate to practice!
It does the job but the letter tiles don’t lock in square. My words are crooked. Maybe a better jig or clearer stencil.
The guide never advised sending a small test first. I sent my whole balance and was anxious waiting confirmations.
It’s okay. Would prefer thicker 316 steel, not sure what this is. Doesn’t feel heat/fire resistant enough.
The screwdriver in the kit is junk. The tip rounded off before I got halfway through tightening.
Instructions on "air-gapped" are vague. It says transfer via USB but doesn’t explain how to verify the file or signatures.
Wish the guide emphasized the 3-2-1 backup rule. It’s mentioned in passing but not actionable. I needed concrete steps.
One of the letter trays didn’t include a "Z". I had to flip an "N" and it looks awful.
Good idea, mediocre execution. The metal plate bends if you press hard. A thicker plate or better stamping method would help.
The example seed in the guide uses 24 words, not 25. That’s misleading for Monero. Please fix.
No tamper-evident bag or seal. For a security product, I expected better packaging.
The guide says to verify the address but doesn’t explain how to compare it safely or show checksum tips.
Letters bounce around during stamping and the spacing drifted. Maybe include a clamp or stamp jig.
Paper wallet card feels like cheap stock and smudged with just a sweaty hand. Not durable.
Support answered after 4 days about the broken link. Replacement PDF helped but that delay was stressful.
I expected two plates for redundancy. Only one included and no clear mention of 3 copies in different places.
Guide mentions integrated addresses but doesn’t explain when to use subaddresses. Left me unsure for day-to-day use.
There’s a checksum line in Monero seeds but the booklet didn’t explain what that means. Beginners need context.
Some burrs on the plate holes scratched my finger. Needs deburring and better finish.
It would be better to have a fire test rating or some certification reference for the plate.
The URL for the download page isn’t HTTPS in the print. Shouldn’t a security product model best practices?
